Title: pageContext - Homepage Slug Resolution
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: Execute in GraphiQL UI ({{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql) via playwright browser. No auth required for pageContext query. Store configured with homepage.
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/") { slugInfo { entityInfo { id objectType semanticUrl isActive } redirectUrl } store { storeId storeName catalogId storeUrl defaultLanguage { cultureName } defaultCurrency { code } } whiteLabelingSettings { themePresetName logoUrl } user { id userName } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify all four sub-objects present
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] slugInfo.entityInfo is non-null for homepage permalink
[DATA] slugInfo.entityInfo.objectType is a known type (e.g., "ContentFile" or "Page")
[DATA] slugInfo.entityInfo.isActive = true
[DATA] slugInfo.entityInfo.semanticUrl = "/"
[DATA] slugInfo.redirectUrl is null (no redirect for homepage)
[DATA] store.storeId = {{STORE_ID}}
[DATA] store.storeName is non-empty string
[DATA] store.defaultLanguage.cultureName is non-empty (e.g., "en-US")
[DATA] store.defaultCurrency.code is non-empty (e.g., "USD")
[DATA] whiteLabelingSettings.themePresetName is non-empty string
[DATA] user is present (Anonymous user context when no auth token)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → identical response (deterministic)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; slugInfo null for homepage; store.storeId empty; defaultCurrency or defaultLanguage null; whiteLabelingSettings null; response panel blank or crash

Title: pageContext - CMS Page Permalink + slugInfo Category Resolution
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; CMS pages exist (e.g., /contacts); catalog categories exist. NOTE: pageContext.permalink resolves CMS pages (ContentFile); catalog categories/products resolve via separate slugInfo(slug:) query.
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with CMS page permalink: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "contacts") { slugInfo { entityInfo { id objectType semanticUrl isActive } redirectUrl } store { storeId } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify CMS page resolves via pageContext
[ACT] Clear editor → type slugInfo query for category: query { slugInfo(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" slug: "printers") { entityInfo { id objectType semanticUrl isActive } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify category resolves via slugInfo
Assertions:
[DATA] CMS page: slugInfo.entityInfo.objectType = "ContentFile"
[DATA] CMS page: slugInfo.entityInfo.id is non-empty
[DATA] CMS page: slugInfo.entityInfo.semanticUrl = "contacts"
[DATA] CMS page: slugInfo.entityInfo.isActive = true
[DATA] CMS page: store.storeId = {{STORE_ID}} (store context present)
[DATA] Category: entityInfo.objectType = "Category"
[DATA] Category: entityInfo.id is non-empty
[DATA] Category: entityInfo.isActive = true
[DATA] Each entity type resolves via the correct query (pageContext for CMS pages, slugInfo for catalog)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute each query → same entityInfo returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: CMS page entityInfo null for known /contacts permalink; objectType wrong; category not resolved via slugInfo; errors[] in response; isActive = false for active entity

Title: pageContext - Store Settings in Response
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; store configured with known settings
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/") { store { storeId storeName storeUrl defaultLanguage { cultureName twoLetterLanguageName } defaultCurrency { code symbol } availableLanguages { cultureName } availableCurrencies { code } settings { emailVerificationEnabled emailVerificationRequired anonymousUsersAllowed seoLinkType } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify settings match expected store configuration
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] store.settings.emailVerificationEnabled is boolean
[DATA] store.settings.emailVerificationRequired is boolean
[DATA] store.settings.anonymousUsersAllowed is boolean
[DATA] store.settings.seoLinkType is non-empty string
[DATA] store.availableLanguages is non-empty array with at least one language
[DATA] store.availableCurrencies is non-empty array with at least one currency
[DATA] store.defaultLanguage.cultureName matches one of availableLanguages entries
[DATA] store.defaultCurrency.code matches one of availableCurrencies entries
[DATA] store.storeUrl is a valid URL string or non-empty
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute → same settings returned (deterministic)
[ADMIN] Admin > Stores > {{STORE_ID}} settings match the returned values
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: settings null; emailVerificationEnabled missing; availableLanguages empty; defaultLanguage not in availableLanguages list; errors[] in response

Title: pageContext - Authenticated vs Anonymous User Context
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; user credentials available for authenticated test
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Ensure Headers tab is empty (no auth token) → click query editor
[ACT] Type query: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/") { user { id userName email roles { name } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — record anonymous user context
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{USER_EMAIL}}&password={{USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token
[ACT] Click "Headers" button (right tab) → set auth token via execCommand('insertText') on Headers textarea (see graphiql-interaction.md §2 — only step using JS) → take screenshot to verify token visible in Headers panel (not Variables)
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type same query: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/") { user { id userName email roles { name } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — compare authenticated user context vs anonymous
Assertions:
[DATA] Anonymous: user.userName is null or "Anonymous" or similar anonymous identifier
[DATA] Anonymous: user.email is null or empty (no PII exposed for anonymous)
[DATA] Anonymous: user.roles is null or empty array (no elevated permissions)
[DATA] Authenticated: user.userName = {{USER_EMAIL}} or account username
[DATA] Authenticated: user.email = {{USER_EMAIL}}
[DATA] Authenticated: user.id is non-empty string (different from anonymous id)
[DATA] Authenticated: user.roles is non-empty (at least one role assigned)
[DATA] No cross-contamination: anonymous query does NOT return authenticated user data
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Remove auth header → re-execute → back to anonymous context
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Authenticated user data returned without auth token (P0 data leak); anonymous user has email populated; authenticated user has empty id; roles populated for anonymous; errors[] in response

Title: pageContext - Invalid Permalink Returns Null SlugInfo
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; no auth required
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with invalid permalink: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "this-page-definitely-does-not-exist-xyz-999") { slugInfo { entityInfo { id objectType semanticUrl isActive } redirectUrl } store { storeId } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify graceful null handling
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with empty permalink: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "") { slugInfo { entityInfo { id objectType } } store { storeId } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify empty permalink handling
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data (not crash or 500)
[DATA] Invalid permalink: slugInfo.entityInfo is null (no entity for this URL)
[DATA] Invalid permalink: slugInfo.redirectUrl is null (no redirect configured)
[DATA] Invalid permalink: store still returned with valid storeId (store context unaffected)
[DATA] Empty permalink: similar graceful handling (null entityInfo or root page)
[DATA] No stack traces or internal paths in response
[ERRORS] No top-level errors[] in response (invalid permalink is not an error — just null slug)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute valid permalink ("/") → entityInfo returns correctly (query still functional)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: 500 error or GraphiQL crash for invalid permalink; top-level errors[] instead of null slugInfo; stack trace in response; store context null due to invalid permalink; GraphiQL frozen after invalid query

Title: pageContext - Organization-Scoped White Labeling Settings
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; organization with custom branding exists; organizationId known; auth header set (org member)
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; organizationId=known org with custom branding; user_email={{USER_EMAIL}}; user_password={{USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{USER_EMAIL}}&password={{USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click "Headers" button (right tab) → set auth token via execCommand('insertText') on Headers textarea (see graphiql-interaction.md §2 — only step using JS) → take screenshot to verify token visible in Headers panel (not Variables)
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query without organizationId: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/") { whiteLabelingSettings { themePresetName logoUrl secondaryLogoUrl faviconUrl footerLinks { title url } mainMenuLinks { title url } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — record default (store-level) white labeling settings
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with organizationId: query { pageContext(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" permalink: "/" organizationId: "<ORG_ID>") { whiteLabelingSettings { themePresetName logoUrl secondaryLogoUrl faviconUrl footerLinks { title url } mainMenuLinks { title url } organizationId } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — compare org-scoped vs default white labeling
Assertions:
[DATA] Default query: whiteLabelingSettings reflects store-level branding
[DATA] Org-scoped query: whiteLabelingSettings.organizationId = <ORG_ID> (if org has custom branding)
[DATA] If org has custom logo: logoUrl differs from default store logoUrl
[DATA] If org has custom theme: themePresetName may differ from store default
[DATA] mainMenuLinks and footerLinks may differ between store and org scope
[DATA] Org-scoped query does NOT return another organization's branding
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute without organizationId → back to store-level branding
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Org-scoped query returns store branding (org override not applied); org branding returned without organizationId (leaking); errors[] when organizationId passed; wrong org's branding returned
